title: STAT+: Hospitals offer chatbots to fight off ChatGPT
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: Hospitals offer their own chatbots, Medicare's ACCESS pilot, and an update Utah's 'AI doctor' experiment.

title: FDA Issues Draft Guidance on Genome Editing Safety Standards to Advance Gene Therapy Development
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a draft guidance for sponsors seeking approval of human gene therapy products involving genome editing technologies.

title: For Ben Sasse, Revolution Medicines’ pancreatic cancer trial felt like his best, only option
summary: Former Sen. Ben Sasse gives a glimpse of what it's like to be a participant in an tough but promising new drug for pancreatic cancer.

title: A top WHO official confronts Iran war fallout, and weighs bid for an even bigger role
summary: The director of WHO's Eastern Mediterranean region is weighing whether to run to be the next director-general at a time of tumult over Iran and beyond.

title: STAT+: Dana-Farber CEO talks untangling from Mass General Brigham and building new cancer hospital
summary: Dana-Farber CEO Benjamin Ebert has no shortage of ideas and vision for the cancer institute as it untangles from Mass General Brigham and builds a new hospital.

title: Opinion: My brother can’t access a just-approved breakthrough drug for his rare disease
summary: Rare disease clinical trials, policy discussions, and advocacy initiatives often leave adults behind.

title: STAT+: Maryland state affordability board sets its first price cap for a medicine
summary: The board will now oversee a process to lower the cost of the type 2 diabetes medicine Jardiance by January 2027.

title: STAT+: FDA pushes drugmakers to report missing clinical trial results
summary: FDA officials said an internal analysis found results were not submitted for nearly 30% of studies “highly likely” to fall under mandatory reporting requirements.

title: STAT+: Federal judge tosses California No Surprises Act lawsuit against HaloMD
summary: HaloMD is a middleman that became the top filer of out-of-network billing disputes on behalf of medical providers.

title: STAT+: Access granted: CMS greenlights more than 150 participants for chronic care experiment
summary: CMS greenlights more than 150 digital health companies for its ACCESS experiment, a new program for tech-backed chronic care with outcome-aligned payments.

title: Spyre drug for inflammatory bowel disease shows promise in early study
summary: The therapy, which works similarly to Takeda’s blockbuster Entyvio, showed remission rates that suggest a “best-in-class” profile, the company said.

summary: President Trump vowed to take on health insurers, but his policies tell a different story.

title: Allogene data suggest ‘off-the-shelf’ CAR-T could delay relapse in lymphoma
summary: Initial results from a high-stakes trial indicate the biotech may have finally found a niche in lymphoma care for a donor-derived cell therapy.

summary: The subject of buyout rumors this year, Revolution posted Phase 3 results one analyst called a “game changer” in a tough-to-treat tumor.

summary: Metastatic pancreatic cancer patients who received a targeted pill from Revolution Medicines lived nearly twice as long as patients who got chemotherapy

summary: In a trial, metastatic pancreatic cancer patients on the medicine lived twice as long as those on chemotherapy.

summary: The new data bolster Allogene’s efforts to develop a new, easily administered cell therapy that could delay or prevent cancer recurrence.

title: FDA again spurns Replimune melanoma drug
summary: In a statement late Friday, CEO Sushil Patel disputed the FDA’s criticisms of the company and said Replimune had received “inconsistent communications.” It’ll be forced to cut jobs as well.

title: Oricell closes a ‘pre-IPO’ megaround to aim CAR-T at solid tumors
summary: The Shanghai-based biotech has a cell therapy that, if successful in testing, could become the first treatment of its kind for liver cancer.

title: RFK Jr. rewrites ACIP rules; Gilead, Roche dig into protein degraders
summary: The newly published charter widens the required expertise to become an ACIP member and stresses vaccine safety. Elsewhere, Gilead licensed a Kymera drug and a new RNA-focused startup emerged.

title: Women have awaited a revolution in menopause. It hasn’t arrived.
summary: Persistent barriers are still hindering drug development for a host of menopause symptoms.

title: Unlocking biological barriers: nonclinical safety considerations for RNA-based complex biologics
Invivyd reveals plans to test an antibody drug against measles
summary: The drug, which Invivyd sees as a potential treatment and “targeted alternative” for the vaccine-hesitant, comes as measles cases in the U.S. are reaching levels not seen in decades.
